title: Discovery Park Seattle | Trails, Lighthouse & Visitor Guide 2026
content: # Discovery Park Seattle Guide: Trails, Lighthouse, Parking & Best Views. Picture this: I’m standing on a windswept bluff. Below, waves crash against a wild beach leading to a historic lighthouse. This isn’t a remote wilderness hours from civilization—it’s Discovery Park Seattle, tucked within Seattle’s city limits. As Seattle’s largest park, I’ve discovered Discovery Park defies expectations. Where else can I move from dense Pacific Northwest forest to open meadows to dramatic sea cliffs within a single afternoon? Whether you’re a first-time visitor planning your Seattle itinerary, a local seeking weekend adventure, or a nature photographer chasing that perfect shot, Discovery Park delivers experiences that rival destinations requiring far more travel time. ## **What Makes Discovery Park Seattle Special**? I’ve found Discovery Park isn’t just the biggest Seattle park—it’s a masterclass in ecosystem diversity packed into one remarkable location. The park serves as Seattle’s most significant wildlife refuge. The story of how Discovery Park came to exist is as compelling as the park itself.

title: Discovery Park — Park Review | Condé Nast Traveler
content: Perched on the quiet shores of the Puget Sound in Seattle's Magnolia neighborhood, this is the city's biggest park. Though nearly every square foot of Discovery Park is impressive, don't miss an opportunity to learn about this lands' rich past and present history at the United Indians of All Tribes' Daybreak Star Cultural Center. Indigenous Duwamish and other Coast Salish peoples have resided in the Seattle area for centuries, and this center provides a few ways to learn more about this, including a permanent art collection. Thanks to the well-maintained trails and markers, this park is fairly easy to navigate. Downloading a map on the park's website is a good idea to have on-hand. If you're short on time, the 2.8-mile Loop Trail offers a best-hits list (it's also one of the easier to accomplish hikes in the area). $$$ | United States, Seattle, 99 Union St. By most any measure, Seattle’s top hotel.
